what_it_does: Implements a token-driven atomic React component kit and two generic renderers: AtomNode turns a JSON atom tree into React elements, and WidgetHost renders registered widgets either as React components or atom trees, using shared CSS custom-property tokens.
observations: Defines TOK helper mapping CSS custom properties to color, radius, font, and background values.
Provides atomic components Text, Glyph, Swatch, Tag, Bar, MediaTile, AtomButton and molecular layout components Stack, Row, Grid, Panelet.
AtomNode interprets declarative node specs with atom/props/children, including inline strings and a fallback for unknown atoms.
WidgetHost looks up type in window.MS_REGISTRY, validates props via REG.validate, and chooses between spec.render React component, spec.tree, or an inline tree.
Exposes window.AtomNode, window.WidgetHost, and window.MS_ATOMS for cross-module or agent use.
AtomButton and WidgetHost use an onEmit callback pattern for action propagation.
MediaTile supports image, video, and placeholder states with external class names mock-thumb and mock-thumb__overlay.
standouts: AtomNode makes a JSON atom spec a first-class render target, enabling dynamically generated components.
WidgetHost unifies hand-coded React widgets and declarative atom trees behind a single registry lookup.
The file explicitly names an atomic-design hierarchy: atoms, molecules, organisms.
conventions: Functional React components with inline style objects and default parameter values.
Centralized visual tokens read from CSS custom properties.
Global window exports for shared runtime access.
Declarative tree shape using atom, props, and children keys.
Default sizing and gap values (e.g., gap=10, size=13, height=96).
concerns: Relies on undeclared globals React, Icon, Chip, Btn, and window.MS_REGISTRY without imports or existence checks.
Validation failures are noted in a comment but not surfaced to the UI or console in the shown code.
No type information or runtime prop-type checks; correctness depends on external REG.validate behavior.
MediaTile references class names whose styles are not defined in this file.
AtomNode uses array index as React key for children, which may cause reconciliation issues for dynamic trees.
notes: This file is the rendering glue of the design system: a small atom vocabulary plus a host that can render both concrete React widgets and agent-generated declarative component trees.
questions: Where are Icon, Chip, Btn, and window.MS_REGISTRY defined and what are their exact interfaces?
What schema shape and validation result format does REG.validate return?
Which stylesheet or theme file defines the CSS custom properties that TOK references?
Is AtomNode intended to be the sole rendering path, or will spec.render components also compose these atoms?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it provides atomic components and a WidgetHost that renders registered widgets via React components or declarative atom trees.
intention_signals: It appears designed to let both human-authored and LLM-generated widgets coexist in one system by reducing every UI structure to a small, style-token-backed atom vocabulary rendered through a common host.
novelty: The pairing of AtomNode with WidgetHost elevates a JSON atom tree to a peer of a React component, so new widgets can be generated and rendered without writing or loading additional JSX modules.
contribution: A reusable atom vocabulary and a host abstraction that lets the fused system render hand-built React widgets and dynamically generated declarative component trees from a shared registry, all styled through common tokens.
summary_for_embedding: atoms.jsx defines a CSS-token-based atomic React component library, a declarative AtomNode renderer for JSON atom trees, and a WidgetHost that renders registered widgets via React components or atom specs.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a shared, style-token-driven rendering layer so hand-built and dynamically generated widgets can be rendered consistently through a registry and a declarative atom format.

symbols.does: Renders styled text using design tokens.
symbols.description: Text is a flexible typography atom that accepts size, weight, color, display font flag, margin top, and additional inline styles, defaulting to a 13px regular ink-colored body font.

symbols.does: Renders an icon, optionally inside a boxed badge.
symbols.description: Glyph displays an Icon by name with configurable size and color; when a box size is supplied, it centers the icon in a rounded gold-tinted badge.
Swatch draws a rounded square of the given color with a subtle inset border, defaulting to 22px.
Tag wraps children in a Chip component, mapping its tone prop to the Chip kind prop.
Bar shows a value between 0 and 1 as a filled rounded bar against a muted track, using a configurable tone.

symbols.does: Renders an image, video, or placeholder media thumbnail.
symbols.description: MediaTile displays an img or video when src is provided, otherwise shows a placeholder icon; it can overlay a label using mock-thumb class names.

symbols.does: Renders a button that emits an action.
symbols.description: AtomButton renders a Btn with optional icon and kind, invoking onEmit with either the provided emit object or a default action keyed by the label.

symbols.does: Renders children in a vertical flex column.
symbols.description: Stack is a layout molecule that arranges children vertically with a configurable gap and inline styles.

symbols.does: Renders children in a horizontal flex row.
symbols.description: Row is a layout molecule that arranges children horizontally with configurable gap, wrapping, alignment, and inline styles.

symbols.does: Renders children in a CSS grid.
symbols.description: Grid is a layout molecule that places children into a fixed number of equal columns with a configurable gap.

symbols.does: Renders a card-like container with an optional title.
symbols.description: Panelet wraps children in a quiet card with padding and rounded corners, optionally rendering a small uppercase title above the content.
